title: Examining objects with electric fields
abstract: Objects are examined using electric fields (1109) created by a first set of electrodes (1101 to 1108). A selected electrode (1108) from the first set is energized as a transmitter and a different selected electrode (1107) is monitored as a receiver, to establish a capacitively coupled pair of electrodes during a coupling operation (912, 913). Two electrodes (1101, 1102) are selected that are separated by a first distance as a first coupled pair during a first coupling operation. During a second coupling operation, two electrodes (1101, 1103) are selected that are separated by a second distance, where the second distance is larger than the first distance. The first coupled pair and the second coupled pair both include a first electrode in common (1101).
claims:
Claims 1. A method of examining objects using electric fields created by a first set of electrodes, in which: a selected electrode from said first set is energized as a transmitter; and a different selected electrode is monitored as a receiver, to establish a capacitively coupled pair of electrodes defining a coupling operation, comprising the steps of: selecting two electrodes (1 101 , 1 102) of said first set separated by a first distance (1203) as a first coupled pair, during a first coupling operation (912); and selecting two electrodes (1101 , 1103) separated by a second distance (1204) as a second coupled pair, during a second coupling operation (913), characterized in that: said second distance is larger than said first distance; and said first coupled pair and said second coupled pair both include a first electrode in common (1101 ). 2. The method of claim 1 , further comprising the step of sequentially selecting further coupled pairs of electrodes (1101 , 1 104; 1 101 , 1 105) from said first set during respective coupling operations (914, 915), wherein each said further coupled pair includes said first electrode in common (1101). 3. The method of claim 2, wherein said first electrode in common maintains the same functionality during all coupling operations, as an energised-transmitter electrode or as a monitored-receiver electrode. 4. The method of claim 2 or claim 3, further comprising the step of sequentially selecting further coupled pairs of electrodes (1108, 1 107; 1 108, 1 106) from said first set during respective sensing operations, each including a second electrode in common (1108). 

 5 The method of claim 4, wherein said first set of electrodes includes a first end (1 101 ) electrode and a second end electrode (1108), wherein: said first end electrode is selected as said first electrode in common; and said second end electrode is selected as said second electrode in common. 6. The method of any of claims 2 to 5, further comprising the step of sequentially selecting coupled pairs of electrodes from a second set of electrodes (Fig. 20: 1 105,1106; 1105,1107), each including an electrode in common (1105). 7. The method of any of claims 2 to 6, further comprising the step of adjusting the level of energising a selected transmitter electrode dependent upon the distance of a coupled monitored electrode from and said selected transmitter electrode. 8. The method of claim 7, further comprising the step of applying an input pulse to energise a selected transmitter electrode. 9. The method of claim 8, further comprising the step of directing said input pulse to any selected transmitter electrode via a de-multiplexing operation. 10. The method of any of claims 1 to 9, further comprising the steps of: multiplexing output signals received from any selected receiver electrode; conditioning said multiplexed output signals to produce conditioned 

 output signals; and digitizing said conditioned output signals. 11. An apparatus for examining objects (203) using electric fields (1 109, 1201 ), comprising: a first set of electrodes; an energising circuit (403) for energising a selected one of said electrodes as a transmitter; a monitoring circuit (409) for monitoring a selected one of said electrodes as a receiver, such that an energised transmitter electrode and a monitored receiver electrode establish a coupled pair of capacitively coupled electrodes during a coupling operation; and a processor (402), wherein said processor is configured to: select two of said electrodes (1101 , 1 102) separated by a first distance (1203) as a first coupled pair during a first coupling operation (912); and select two of said electrodes (1 101 , 1 103) separated by a second distance (1204) as a second coupled pair during a second coupling operation (913); characterized in that: said second distance is larger than said first distance; and said first coupled pair and said second coupled pair both include a first electrode in common (1 101). 12. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein said processor is also configured to: sequentially select further coupled pairs of electrodes (1101 , 1 103; 1 101 , 1 104) from said first set during respective coupling operations, each including said first electrode in common (1 101). 13. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein said processor is further configured to sequentially select further coupled pairs of electrodes (1108, 1 107; 1 108, 1 106) during respective coupling operations, wherein each said further coupled pair includes a second electrode in common (1108). 14. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein said first set comprises between four and eight electrodes, having a first end electrode (1101 ) and a second end electrode (1108). 15. The apparatus of claim 4, wherein said processor is configured to: select said first end electrode as said first electrode in common; and select said second end electrode as said second electrode in common.
